source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two rays starting in $S$ form a right angle. More rays starting in $S$ are drawn within the right angle so that each angle $10^{\circ}, 20^{\circ}, 30^{\circ}, 40^{\circ}, 50^{\circ}, 60^{\circ}, 70^{\circ}$ and $80^{\circ}$ is enclosed by two rays. What is the minimum number of rays that have to be drawn? <image1>

image_dependency: question_text fully specifies the geometry in words using rays and angle measures
an image is registered, but the task appears solvable from the textual description alone
multi_step: the problem asks for a minimum number of rays, indicating an optimization/combinatorial reasoning component
solver must coordinate multiple target angles (10° through 80°) within a 90° configuration rather than compute a single direct value
the setup involves relationships among several rays and enclosed angles, which is more than a one-step arithmetic lookup
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value: 3
the target quantity is clearly defined as the minimum number of rays to be drawn
the solution can be checked by constructing or validating a ray set that realizes all listed angles and confirming minimality

decision_reasons: Assets are complete: question, answer, and image are all present with no registry issues.
Verifiability is high because the target is a clearly defined numeric answer.
The problem has moderate multi-step reasoning value and no risk flags.
Text appears sufficient to preserve the core semantics even without heavy image dependence.
